content.claim: Hungary's overall public education spending is relatively low (4.1% of government expenditure, ranking 41/42 in OECD/partner comparison, 2022), and intended instruction time for lower secondary students ranks 32/34 among OECD countries (796 hours, 2023), making internal resource allocation between selective and comprehensive tracks more consequential.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Low ranking is a comparative fact but does not directly measure the fiscal impact of the 6/8-year track itself
Instruction-time differences may reflect curriculum structure rather than funding constraints
The inference that this context makes internal tracking allocation 'more consequential' is analytical rather than empirically demonstrated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
